title: pyrus duplex
intro: Pyrus duplex, also known as the double-flowered pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family and is native to Asia. The plant's bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Pyrus duplex include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory function, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Pyrus duplex can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to digestive problems and skin conditions.
